goal: Build a tiny arithmetic DSL in Python at calc/. Required pieces: calc/lexer.py (tokenizes a source string into number, identifier, operator, and paren tokens), calc/parser.py (builds an AST supporting integer literals, the four binary operators + - * /, parentheses, variable references, and let-binding 'let x = expr in expr'), calc/interpreter.py exposing eval_program(source: str) -> int, and calc/__init__.py re-exporting eval_program. Also write three example programs at examples/ex1.calc, examples/ex2.calc, examples/ex3.calc that exercise: ex1 = simple arithmetic with parens, ex2 = a let-binding, ex3 = nested let-bindings. Finally, write a runner script run_examples.py that calls eval_program on each example file and prints '<filename>: <result>' lines. Run run_examples.py at the end.

name: mini-calc-dsl
goal: Build a tiny arithmetic DSL in Python at calc/. Required pieces: calc/lexer.py (tokenizes a source string into number, identifier, operator, and paren tokens), calc/parser.py (builds an AST supporting integer literals, the four binary operators + - * /, parentheses, variable references, and let-binding 'let x = expr in expr'), calc/interpreter.py exposing eval_program(source: str) -> int, and calc/__init__.py re-exporting eval_program. Also write three example programs at examples/ex1.calc, examples/ex2.calc, examples/ex3.calc that exercise: ex1 = simple arithmetic with parens, ex2 = a let-binding, ex3 = nested let-bindings. Finally, write a runner script run_examples.py that calls eval_program on each example file and prints '<filename>: <result>' lines. Run run_examples.py at the end.

Run benchmark.py as the final step so REPORT.md is produced.

name: sort-algo-comparison-report
goal: Implement three sort algorithms in Python — bubble sort, merge sort, and quicksort — each in its own module file (bubble.py, merge.py, quicksort.py), each exposing a sort(lst) -> list function that does NOT mutate the input. Also write benchmark.py that generates random integer lists at sizes 100, 1000, and 5000 (using a fixed random seed of 42 for reproducibility), runs each sort algorithm against each size, measures wall time with time.perf_counter, and writes a Markdown report at REPORT.md. The report must include: an introduction, a table of measured times (algorithm × size), at least one paragraph of observations (e.g. about complexity, surprises, or fairness of the comparison), and a conclusion paragraph. Run benchmark.py as the final step so REPORT.md is produced.

name: static-blog-site
goal: Build a small static blog under site/ consisting of: site/index.html (the home page listing all posts), site/posts/post-1.html, site/posts/post-2.html, site/posts/post-3.html (three blog posts on any topic), and site/style.css (a single shared stylesheet). Each post page must have a level-1 header with the post title, a date paragraph, the post body, and a 'Back to home' link to ../index.html. The home page must list all three posts as links. style.css must be referenced by every page and must define at least colors and a readable body font. No JavaScript.
